steps.title: Use the right-triangle shortcut from the figure
steps.content:
The right-angle mark at $B$ means $\angle ABC=90^\circ$. Since $A$, $B$, and $C$ are on the circle, the side opposite that right angle, $AC$, is a diameter. The diameter is $145$, so:
$$AD+CD=AC=145$$

steps.title: Apply the altitude-to-hypotenuse theorem
steps.content:
Segment $BD$ is perpendicular to $AC$, so $BD$ is the altitude from the right angle to the hypotenuse. For a right triangle, the altitude satisfies:
$$BD^2=AD\cdot CD$$
Given $BD=\sqrt{286}$, this becomes:
$$AD\cdot CD=(\sqrt{286})^2=286$$

steps.title: Solve for the two hypotenuse pieces
steps.content:
Let $AD=x$. Then $CD=145-x$, so:
$$x(145-x)=286$$
$$x^2-145x+286=0$$
The two factors are $2$ and $143$, since $2+143=145$ and $2\cdot143=286$. Thus the two segment lengths are $2$ and $143$.

steps.title: Use AB < BC to choose the order
steps.content:
In this right-triangle setup, the shorter leg is next to the shorter hypotenuse segment. Since $AB$$r=\frac{CD}{AD}=\frac{143}{2}=71.5$$
The answer is 71.5.
